title: Teacher — Enrolment
content:
Overview


The Enrolment page is where teachers manage student enrolment requests for their subjects. Students browse available subjects and submit requests through their Student Portal. Teachers review, approve, or reject those requests here. The page is organized into three collapsible sections: Pending Requests, Enrolled, and Rejected.



Filters and Navigation


At the top of the Enrolment page, teachers can filter what they see:


  • Term filter: View enrolments by term (Fall, Spring, Winter, Summer, September Term, January Term, May Term, or All Terms)
  • Year selector: Plus/minus buttons to change the academic year
  • Search: Filter by student name or subject name

A badge on the page title shows the number of pending requests waiting for action.



Pending Requests


This section lists all enrolment requests that have not yet been approved or rejected. Each request shows:


  • Student name
  • Subject they want to join
  • Date the request was submitted

To process a request:


  1. Click Approve to accept the student into the subject — they will appear in the Enrolled section and in the gradebook
  2. Click Reject to deny the request — the student will see the rejected status in their own Enrolment page

The Pending section can be collapsed by clicking the header. A chevron icon indicates whether it's expanded or collapsed.



Enrolled Section


Lists all students who have been approved and are currently enrolled in the teacher's subjects. Each entry shows:


  • Student name
  • Subject they are enrolled in
  • Enrolment date

This section is useful for verifying which students are in which subject before sending assignments or entering grades. It can also be collapsed.



Rejected Section


Lists all enrolment requests that have been rejected. Each entry shows:


  • Student name
  • Subject they requested
  • Rejection date

Rejected students can re-apply by submitting a new enrolment request from their Student Portal. This section can also be collapsed and is hidden by default to reduce clutter.



How Students Submit Requests


Students browse available subjects through the Enrolment page in their Student Portal. They see all subjects created by teachers at their school, filtered by term and year. To request enrolment:


  1. Student finds a subject in the Available Courses list
  2. Clicks Request Enrolment
  3. The request appears in the teacher's Pending Requests section
  4. Student can track the status in their My Enrolment Requests section (Pending, Enrolled, or Rejected)

Students can also delete their own rejected requests if they wish to clean up their enrolment history.



Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "Pending requests are at the top — you'll see a badge with the count on the page title."
  • "You can collapse any section by clicking its header if you want to focus on one list at a time."
  • "Students can re-apply if rejected — they just submit a new request from their Student Portal."
  • "Use the term and year filters to see enrolments for a specific semester."
  • "If a student says they requested enrolment but you don't see it, check that you're both looking at the same term and year."
